title: message_set update: 'body' ownership check uses notFound instead of forbidden for non-owner
description: jmap.rs message_set update path checks sender_id == my_pub_id, and on mismatch returns notFound (not forbidden). This is RFC 8620-compliant (hiding existence from non-owners), but the behavior is surprising: a caller who knows the exact message ID but does not own it gets a notFound response, which could lead them to incorrectly believe the message was deleted rather than that access was denied. This is a deliberate design choice but is not commented, making it a future-maintainer trap. A comment explaining the deliberate notFound-not-forbidden choice would prevent future 'bug' reports.
close_reason: Fixed in round 34: body cap, forbidden error, unknown patch paths, description limit
